questions.question: Explain the main difference between the copy-on-write (COW) and copy-on-read (COR) approaches to handling shared files in a multi-user operating system.
questions.hint: Describe the implications of each approach on system performance and data consistency.

questions.question: Suppose a file system implementation uses copy-on-write for a particular file. If a write operation is performed on that file, what happens to the original file, and what is the purpose of the copy?
questions.hint: Focus on the file system's behavior when handling modifications to the file.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the advantages and disadvantages of using copy-on-write in a file system, considering factors such as concurrency, system performance, and data consistency.
questions.hint: Provide a detailed analysis of the trade-offs involved in implementing copy-on-write.
